Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 4:

Out of curiosity, is there a reason this patch was rebased?  It doesn't have any code changes, and was previously reviewed and had passed all the testing.

Patch Set 4:

Ben, any chance you could inspect this series so it can be landed?

Patch Set 4:

Actually I set lsi_kobj with kobject_get() to increase the reference. This was to avoid potentially having lsi_kobj disappear underneath us.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as db67e686d9abcf750359820bfbdb754ab611bf5c by John L. Hammond

Patch Set 5:

BTW, James, Because of how gerrit works I think you'll have to push a new change (with a new Change-Id) to get this landed. Then the next change in your series will need to be rebased onto the new change.

But you should at least try to push a new revision with the same Change-Id and see what happens.

I'm really sorry for the inconvenience.

commit.subject: LU-8066 llite: replace ll_process_config with class_modify_config
commit.message:
LU-8066 llite: replace ll_process_config with class_modify_config

The current method of handling tunables with ll_process_config can
not work with sysfs. So replace ll_process_config handling with
class_modify_config() which can handle sysfs, debugfs and procfs.

Change-Id: I40611930ab2b769c0661aa7dce0c7dd0f2d90204
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32495
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Ben Evans <bevans@cray.com>
